meta.description: Browser-use plugin E2E test cases. Tests verify Claude can accomplish browser automation tasks using the browser-use MCP server tools (18 tools). Requires Chrome/Chromium installed and browser-use Python package.
meta.notes: v3.0: Strengthened checks per multimodel review. Added response_contains_all for behavioral assertions (not just call counts). Added missing tool coverage: browser_type (13), extract_content (14), run_script (15). Replaced vague response_contains_any with specific behavioral assertions. Total: 15 test cases.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a URL, take screenshot, verify page loaded with correct title, close session
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Do the following:
1. Call browser_navigate with url="https://example.com" (do not pass a session_id — let the server create a new one).
2. Record the session_id returned by the navigate call.
3. Call browser_get_state with the session_id to verify the page loaded.
4. Take a screenshot using browser_screenshot, passing the session_id.
5. Call browser_close_session with the session_id.
6. Report the EXACT current URL and page title from get_state.

Always close the session when done. You MUST report the exact URL and title values.

test_cases.description: Regression: two sequential navigate calls must both succeed — no SingletonLock timeout
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Run two sequential navigation tasks. This tests that a second browser session can start even when one is already running:
1. Call browser_navigate with url="https://example.com" and no session_id. Record session_id_A.
2. Call browser_navigate with url="https://example.org" and no session_id. Record session_id_B.
3. Call browser_get_state with session_id_A — report the exact URL.
4. Call browser_get_state with session_id_B — report the exact URL.
5. Close session_id_A with browser_close_session.
6. Close session_id_B with browser_close_session.
7. Confirm both sessions returned different session IDs and both URLs loaded correctly.

FAILURE CRITERIA: If either navigate call times out, returns an error, or get_state shows the wrong URL, this test FAILS. Report 'TEST PASSED' only if both sessions worked correctly with correct URLs, otherwise report 'TEST FAILED' with the error.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a page, get DOM state, verify exact URL, title, and element count are reported
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Do the following:
1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Call browser_get_state with the session_id to get the current page DOM state.
3. From the get_state response, extract and report:
   a. The EXACT current URL (copy it verbatim)
   b. The EXACT page title (copy it verbatim)
   c. The number of interactive elements found
4. Close the session with browser_close_session.

You MUST copy the exact URL and title strings from the get_state response, not guess them.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a page, extract raw HTML, verify specific markup content is present
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script. Do the following: 1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id. 2. Call browser_get_html with the session_id to get the full page HTML source. 3. From the HTML response, verify and report: a. The exact text content inside the

tag b. Whether a

tag exists containing the word 'illustrative' c. Whether the HTML contains a link to 'iana.org' 4. Close the session with browser_close_session. Quote the actual h1 content from the HTML.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a long page, scroll down, verify scroll position changed via get_state
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Do the following:
1. Navigate to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_browser using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Call browser_get_state to capture the initial page state (note the scroll position or visible content).
3. Scroll down 2000 pixels using browser_scroll with direction="down" and amount=2000.
4. Call browser_get_state again to capture state after scrolling.
5. Compare the two states: the visible elements or content should differ because we scrolled.
6. Scroll back up using browser_scroll with direction="up" and amount=2000.
7. Call browser_get_state one final time.
8. Close the session with browser_close_session.
9. Report 'SCROLL VERIFIED' if the get_state results showed different content at different scroll positions. Report 'SCROLL FAILED' if all three states showed the same content.

The key assertion is that get_state returns DIFFERENT element indices or content at different scroll positions.

test_cases.description: Open multiple tabs, verify tab count is 2, switch tabs, close one, verify tab count is 1
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test multi-tab management:
1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Navigate to https://example.org in a new tab using browser_navigate with the same session_id and new_tab=true.
3. Call browser_list_tabs with the session_id.
4. Count the tabs: report the EXACT number (should be 2). If it is not 2, report 'TAB COUNT WRONG'.
5. Switch to the first tab using browser_switch_tab (use the tab_id from list_tabs).
6. Call browser_get_state to verify you're on example.com (report the exact URL).
7. Close the second tab using browser_close_tab.
8. Call browser_list_tabs again.
9. Report the new tab count (should be 1). If it is not 1, report 'TAB COUNT WRONG'.
10. Close the session with browser_close_session.
11. Report 'TABS VERIFIED: 2 then 1' if both counts were correct.

test_cases.description: Navigate to two pages sequentially, go back, verify URL returned to the first page
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test browser history navigation:
1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Call browser_get_state — report the exact URL (should be https://example.com).
3. Navigate to https://example.org using browser_navigate with the same session_id.
4. Call browser_get_state — report the exact URL (should be https://example.org).
5. Call browser_go_back with the session_id to go back in history.
6. Call browser_get_state — report the exact URL (should be back to https://example.com).
7. Close the session with browser_close_session.
8. Report 'BACK NAVIGATION VERIFIED' if the URL after go_back returned to example.com. Report 'BACK NAVIGATION FAILED' if the URL did not change back.

Report all three URLs exactly as returned by get_state.

test_cases.description: Create multiple sessions, verify session counts at each stage, close individually, verify cleanup
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test session management:
1. Navigate to https://example.com to create session A. Record session_id_A.
2. Navigate to https://example.org to create session B (no session_id — new session). Record session_id_B.
3. Call browser_list_sessions. Count and report: 'SESSIONS AFTER CREATE: N' (should be at least 2).
4. Close session A with browser_close_session.
5. Call browser_list_sessions again. Report: 'SESSIONS AFTER CLOSE A: N' (should be at least 1, and session_id_A should NOT be in the list).
6. Close session B with browser_close_session.
7. Call browser_list_sessions one final time. Report: 'SESSIONS AFTER CLOSE B: N' (should be 0 or only other sessions).
8. Report 'SESSION LIFECYCLE VERIFIED' if counts decreased correctly at each step.

Always clean up all sessions.

test_cases.description: Export session state to file, close session, import into new session, verify page loads
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test session persistence:
1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record session_id_A.
2. Export the session using browser_export_session with session_id_A and output_path="/tmp/browser-use-e2e-session.json".
3. Close session_id_A with browser_close_session.
4. Import the session file using browser_import_session with import_path="/tmp/browser-use-e2e-session.json" and navigate_to="https://example.com".
5. Record the new session_id_B from the import response.
6. Call browser_get_state with session_id_B — report the exact URL (should be https://example.com).
7. Close session_id_B with browser_close_session.
8. Confirm session_id_A and session_id_B are DIFFERENT session IDs.
9. Report 'EXPORT/IMPORT VERIFIED' if the import created a new working session. Report 'EXPORT/IMPORT FAILED' if the import failed or get_state showed wrong URL.

Clean up all sessions.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a page, find a link by DOM index, click it, verify the URL changed
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test clicking interactive elements:
1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Call browser_get_state with the session_id to get interactive elements and their indices.
3. Report the URL before clicking: should be https://example.com
4. Find the 'More information...' link in the elements list. Report its index number.
5. Click that link using browser_click with the session_id and the element's index.
6. Call browser_get_state again to get the new URL after clicking.
7. Report the URL after clicking — it should have changed away from example.com (likely to iana.org).
8. Close the session with browser_close_session.
9. Report 'CLICK NAVIGATION VERIFIED' if the URL changed after clicking. Report 'CLICK NAVIGATION FAILED' if the URL stayed the same.

Use index-based clicking (not coordinates).

test_cases.description: Take viewport and full-page screenshots, verify full-page is larger
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Do the following:
1. Navigate to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_browser using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Take a viewport screenshot using browser_screenshot with the session_id (default settings, no full_page flag).
3. Note the screenshot response — look for any size/dimension info.
4. Take a full-page screenshot using browser_screenshot with full_page=true.
5. Note the screenshot response for this one too.
6. Compare the two screenshot responses. The full-page screenshot should capture more content (larger data or dimensions).
7. Close the session with browser_close_session.
8. Report 'FULL PAGE LARGER' if the full-page screenshot returned more data than the viewport screenshot. Report 'SCREENSHOTS SAME SIZE' if they appear identical.

test_cases.description: Test graceful error handling: invalid session operations should return error messages, not crash
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test error handling:
1. Call browser_get_state with session_id="nonexistent_session_12345". Record the EXACT error message returned.
2. Call browser_screenshot with session_id="nonexistent_session_12345". Record the EXACT error message.
3. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate to create a valid session. Record session_id.
4. Close the session with browser_close_session.
5. Try to take a screenshot with the now-closed session_id. Record the EXACT error message.
6. For each of the 3 error calls above, report:
   - 'ERROR 1: [exact message]'
   - 'ERROR 2: [exact message]'
   - 'ERROR 3: [exact message]'
7. Report 'ERRORS HANDLED GRACEFULLY' if all 3 calls returned error messages without crashing. Report 'ERROR HANDLING BROKEN' if any call crashed or threw an unhandled exception.

This tests that the server handles bad input gracefully.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a search engine, type a query into the search box, verify typed text appears
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test text input:
1. Navigate to https://www.google.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Call browser_get_state to find the search input field. Look for a textarea or input element. Report its index.
3. Click the search input element using browser_click with its index.
4. Type the text 'browser automation testing' into the search field using browser_type with the session_id and text parameter.
5. Call browser_get_state again to verify the text was entered — the search field should now contain the typed text.
6. Take a screenshot to visually confirm the text appears in the search box.
7. Close the session with browser_close_session.
8. Report 'TYPE VERIFIED' if the text was successfully entered into the search field. Report 'TYPE FAILED' if the text did not appear in the field.

Do NOT press Enter or submit the form — just type the text.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a content-rich page, use extract_content to get structured text, verify content
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test content extraction:
1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Call browser_extract_content with the session_id to extract the page content as structured text.
3. Report the extracted content — it should contain 'Example Domain' as the heading and descriptive text about this domain being for illustrative examples.
4. Close the session with browser_close_session.
5. Report 'EXTRACTION VERIFIED' if extract_content returned meaningful text content (not empty, not just HTML tags). Report 'EXTRACTION FAILED' if the result was empty or contained only raw HTML.

The extract_content tool should return cleaned text, not raw HTML.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a page, execute JavaScript via run_script, verify the script return value
test_cases.prompt:
You MUST use the browser-use MCP tools (prefixed with 'browser-use' in the tool names, e.g. tools containing 'browser_navigate', 'browser_screenshot', 'browser_get_state', etc. from the browser-use MCP server). Do NOT use chrome-devtools tools. The browser-use tools are: browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_state, browser_extract_content, browser_get_html, browser_screenshot, browser_scroll, browser_go_back, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_list_sessions, browser_close_session, browser_export_session, browser_import_session, browser_run_script.

Test JavaScript execution:
1. Navigate to https://example.com using browser_navigate. Record the session_id.
2. Call browser_run_script with the session_id and script="document.title" to get the page title via JS.
3. Report the return value — it should be 'Example Domain'.
4. Call browser_run_script again with script="document.querySelectorAll('a').length" to count all links on the page.
5. Report the number of links found.
6. Call browser_run_script with script="window.location.href" to get the current URL via JS.
7. Report the URL returned by JavaScript.
8. Close the session with browser_close_session.
9. Report 'SCRIPT EXECUTION VERIFIED' if all three scripts returned correct values (title matches 'Example Domain', link count is a number, URL contains example.com). Report 'SCRIPT EXECUTION FAILED' if any script returned an error or unexpected value.
